title: PoE Powered Device (36–57V Isolated)
description: An isolated flyback converter for a PoE (Power over Ethernet) powered device, operating from the 36–57 V PoE input range to deliver an isolated 5 V/2.5 A (12.5 W) output to the device circuitry.
design_notes: IEEE 802.3af (PoE Class 3) allows up to 15.4 W at the PSE port; accounting for cable loss, the PD receives 12.95 W minimum at a port voltage of 37–57 V. This design operates within Class 3 budget. Isolation is mandatory: the PoE standard requires 1500 Vrms primary-to-secondary isolation in the DC-DC converter to ensure the Cat-5 cable remains safe to touch. The flyback transformer must meet this isolation requirement with adequate creepage and clearance per IEC 60950-1 / IEC 62368-1. The 36–57 V input covers the full classification and operating voltage range; a TVS on the input handles the PoE inrush and transient pulses defined in IEEE 802.3. Output voltage can also be 3.3 V or 12 V depending on the downstream device — adjust Vout and recalculate.
source: TI SLVA107B — Flyback Design; IEEE 802.3af-2003 §33.3; IEC 62368-1 §5.4
